title: Westonia humifusa
intro: Westonia humifusa, also known as West Indian pie plant or wild ginger, is a perennial plant native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The health benefits of Westonia humifusa include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Westonia humifusa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Westonia humifusa is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, often combined with other herbs like ginger and licorice root to enhance its therapeutic effects.
